items.title: NIH Study Links Nurse, Medical Assistant Burnout To Double Shifts, Night Duty
items.summary: An NIH study found that night and double shifts, traumatic incidents, and workplace conflict contribute to burnout among nurses and assistant medical officers in MOH. MOH says it is increasing recruitment to ease workloads and reduce double shifts. The post NIH Study Links Nurse, Medical Assistant Burnout To Double Shifts, Night Duty appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Time For Doctors To Relinquish Management Of The Health Service
items.summary: MOH's historic doctor workforce contraction and 50% no-show for permanent posts are a wake-up call to revamp the health service. Reserve management positions for those qualified in management; doctors should focus on clinical work and building specialties. The post Time For Doctors To Relinquish Management Of The Health Service appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Facing The Silver Tsunami: Why Malaysia Must Overhaul Its Aged Care Infrastructure — Dr Rajeentheran Suntheralingam
items.summary: By 2030, one in six Malaysians will be elderly. By 2040, one in five. The question is not whether we can afford to act. The question is whether we can afford not to. The post Facing The Silver Tsunami: Why Malaysia Must Overhaul Its Aged Care Infrastructure — Dr Rajeentheran Suntheralingam appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Eight-Hour Waits At Miri Hospital’s Emergency Department Amid Doctors’ Shortage
items.summary: Multiple patients complain about waiting for seven to eight hours at Miri Hospital's emergency department for treatment. A doctor says the green zone is sometimes left with only one medical officer on duty or even occasionally has no doctor in charge. The post Eight-Hour Waits At Miri Hospital’s Emergency Department Amid Doctors’ Shortage appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: MOH: Rising API Costs May Push Up Medicine Prices, Private Treatment Costs
items.summary: MOH warns rising API costs could push up medicine prices and private treatment costs, while squeezing local generic drugmakers and distributors. The government’s drug may also come under pressure. The post MOH: Rising API Costs May Push Up Medicine Prices, Private Treatment Costs appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Decentralise Doctor Hiring, Remuneration To Hospital Level In MOH: Bukit Gasing Rep
items.summary: With MOH's "absolute autonomy", Bukit Gasing rep Rajiv moots reforming doctor hiring as an open job marketplace by decentralising recruitment and BIW allowance rates to individual hospitals. Hospital directors will be responsible for hiring and retention. The post Decentralise Doctor Hiring, Remuneration To Hospital Level In MOH: Bukit Gasing Rep appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: JPA Refuses To Further Relax LNPT Requirement For Subspecialty Training
items.summary: JPA denies MOH's request to further relax LNPT requirements for 2026/2027 subspecialty training, citing 6 reasons, including injustice against doctors who didn't apply because they followed the rules. Facilities in public service aren't an absolute right. The post JPA Refuses To Further Relax LNPT Requirement For Subspecialty Training appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Refuting Pharmaniaga, MOH Says Spent More On Generics Than Innovators
items.summary: MOH refutes Pharmaniaga's claim that the government spent more on originators than generics in 2025, clarifying that MOH spent RM3 billion on generics, comprising 78% of its RM3.86 billion drug budget, while RM850 million (22%) was spent on innovators. The post Refuting Pharmaniaga, MOH Says Spent More On Generics Than Innovators appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: MMA Welcomes Greater Operational Autonomy for MOH — MMA
items.summary: Empowering the MOH to manage and deploy its approved positions will enable manpower decisions to be made more efficiently and closer to where health care services are delivered. The post MMA Welcomes Greater Operational Autonomy for MOH — MMA appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Doctor Workforce In Public Sector Shrinks For First Time In A Decade
items.summary: Malaysia’s doctor workforce in the public sector declined in 2024 for the first time since 2014. In an historic contraction, MOH lost 1,444 doctors in 2024 (mostly medical officers and specialists), after over 1,100 MOs rejected permanent posts in 2023. The post Doctor Workforce In Public Sector Shrinks For First Time In A Decade appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Another fall feared at Papar school as Form Two girl reportedly rushed to hospital
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, July 17 — Confirmation on reports that another female student fell and had to be rushed to hospital...
